<!-- Generated by spec-lite | skill: fix | date: 2026-06-12 -->

# Fix Report: Incomplete Content-Type / Header Utilities + DRY Violations

**Date**: 2026-06-12
**Severity**: Medium
**Status**: Fixed

## Symptom

The content-type and header utilities in `packages/core` were incomplete and duplicated:

- `getContentTypeFromFileName` / `getExtensionFromContentType` covered a limited set of MIME types, used two independently-maintained maps that had already drifted (e.g. `.yaml` → `application/x-yaml` in one direction but `application/yaml` → `.yaml` in the other), and missed modern formats (AVIF, WASM, NDJSON, Parquet, Zstandard, etc.).
- `getResponseLanguage` **defaulted unknown content types to text**, so unknown binary payloads were rendered (and copied) as garbled text instead of being offered as a download.
- `getContentTypeForBody` (requestBuilder.ts) was a line-for-line duplicate of `getContentTypeFromBodyMode` (common.ts), and both duplicated the raw-language → content-type map that `getContentTypeFromBody` carried a third copy of.
- `getCommonHeaderNames` was missing modern headers (Forwarded, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, Sec-Fetch-*, Sec-WebSocket-*, rate-limit headers, Idempotency-Key, Priority, …).
- Latent bug found during the refactor: generated response download filenames contained a double dot (`response_<ts>..json`) because `getExtensionFromContentType` returns the leading dot and `getFileName` added another.

## Root Cause

- **File**: `packages/core/src/utils/common.ts`, `packages/core/src/utils/requestBuilder.ts`, `packages/core/src/components/common/ResponseBody.tsx`
- **Cause**: MIME knowledge was scattered across four hand-maintained maps (two in `common.ts`, one each in `requestBuilder.ts` and an inline header scan in `ResponseBody.tsx`) with no single source of truth, so the maps drifted and stayed incomplete. The response classifier assumed text for anything it didn't recognize — unsafe, since at that point the type is truly unknown.

## Fix

1. **Single MIME registry** (`MIME_TYPE_REGISTRY` in `common.ts`): one canonical table of `{ mime, extensions, aliases }` entries from which both `CONTENT_TYPE_BY_EXTENSION` and `EXTENSION_BY_CONTENT_TYPE` are derived at module load. First registry entry wins on extension conflicts (e.g. `.ts` → TypeScript, not MPEG transport stream). Coverage expanded to ~140 canonical types across text, code, data, image, audio, video, archive, font, package, certificate, CAD/3D, and email formats. `.js` now maps to `text/javascript` (RFC 9239), `.yaml`/`.yml` to `application/yaml` (RFC 9512).
2. **`getResponseLanguage`**: parses the MIME type properly (strips parameters, splits type/subtype), recognizes structured-syntax suffixes (`+json`/`+xml`/`+html`/`+csv`), treats all `text/*` and `message/*` plus a curated `TEXT_BASED_SUBTYPES` set (JavaScript, YAML, TOML, GraphQL, NDJSON, JWT, form-urlencoded, …) as text, and **defaults everything else to `binary`**. A missing Content-Type header returns `'none'` (not binary) so empty-body responses (204 etc.) still render as text instead of a download tile; both RequestEditors suppress the content badge for `'none'`.
3. **`getResponseContentType`**: widened to `Record<string, string | string[]>`, resolves multi-value headers to the first value.
4. **DRY**: removed the duplicate `getContentTypeForBody` from `requestBuilder.ts` (call site now uses `getContentTypeFromBodyMode` from `common.ts`); extracted the shared `RAW_LANGUAGE_CONTENT_TYPES` map used by both `getContentTypeFromBodyMode` and the legacy `getContentTypeFromBody`; `ResponseBody.getFileExtension` now delegates to `getResponseContentType` instead of re-scanning headers.
5. **`getCommonHeaderNames`**: added ~35 modern headers (representation/digest, connection, request-context, response-context, security, fetch-metadata, WebSocket, proxy, and rate-limit groups).
6. **Filename bug**: `getFileName` in `ResponseBody.tsx` no longer inserts an extra dot before the extension.

**Why this works**: both lookup directions are now generated from one table so they can never drift; the binary default means unknown payloads are never corrupted by text rendering, while the `'none'` carve-out keeps header-less empty responses displaying as before.

## Regression Test

`packages/core/src/test/utils/common.test.ts` — updated and extended (~25 new assertions/tests):

- `getContentTypeFromFileName`: modern text/code formats (md, yaml, toml, graphql, ndjson, sql, ts, mjs), modern images (avif, heic), archives/binary (xz, zst, jar, wasm, parquet, woff2, pem); `.js` → `text/javascript`.
- `getExtensionFromContentType`: alias resolution (text/xml, application/javascript, application/x-yaml, audio/x-wav, x-zip-compressed, vnd.microsoft.icon), modern types, empty input, and an ext → mime → ext **round-trip consistency test** over common types.
- `getResponseContentType`: multi-value header arrays.
- `getResponseLanguage`: structured suffixes (`problem+json`, `soap+xml`, `svg+xml`), all-`text/*`-is-text, known text-based application types, explicit binary types (png, mp4, pdf, zip, woff2, wasm, protobuf), **unknown types default to binary**, missing header → `'none'`.
- `getContentTypeFromBodyMode` (previously untested): all body modes, raw-language mapping, formdata → null (boundary), file content-type passthrough and octet-stream fallback.
- `getContentTypeFromBody`: csv detection, unknown-language fallback.
- `getCommonHeaderNames`: presence of modern headers + existing no-duplicates test.

## Impact Assessment

- **Blast radius**: response viewing (text vs. download tile), response download filenames/extensions, request Content-Type auto-header, body file upload MIME detection, header autocomplete. Behavior changes: unknown response content types now show the download tile instead of (possibly garbled) text; `.js` uploads send `text/javascript`; `.yaml` uploads send `application/yaml`.
- **Rollback safe**: Yes — pure logic change, no data migrations.
- **Related issues**:
  - Pre-existing unrelated test failure `CollectionsPane.moveDuplicate.test.tsx > moves folder with all nested content preserved` (fails on unmodified code too) — logged in `.spec-lite/TODO.md`.

## Verification

- `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test`: 1825 passed, 1 failed — the failure is the pre-existing `CollectionsPane.moveDuplicate` test, confirmed failing on stashed (unmodified) code.
- `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build`, `wave-client-vscode typecheck`, `@wave-client/web typecheck`: all clean.
